page: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core processing is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core CPU performance and gzip compression, which both fall into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with read-only small block, read-only large block, and write large block benchmarks all ranking in the top 25%. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the r5b.24xlarge is its low single-threaded efficiency and poor performance in text generation tasks, such as LLM inference text generation for a 7B model, which falls into the bottom 10%. Conversely, its high memory density and strong multi-core capabilities make it suitable for memory-intensive workloads. These include large-scale database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that can leverage the 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 96 vCPUs.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor. It delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores at 3.1 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight a significant contrast: single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and gzip compression, rank in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth across both small and large blocks rank in the top 25%. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also perform in the top 25%. Given these characteristics, the r5b.24xlarge is suited for memory-intensive, highly parallel workloads such as large-scale database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is less efficient for single-threaded tasks or LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 768 GB of RAM and 96 vCPUs for high-throughput multi-core workloads and caching.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and high-throughput memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU resources included
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded processing
